# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder:14b)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our legacy Python 2.7 application. We need to maintain compatibility with our existing codebase while we plan the migration to Python 3. Use an older base image that supports Python 2.7 and our legacy dependencies.
# Category: container_security

FROM python:2.7-slim

# Set environment variables
ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE 1
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED 1

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends build-essential libpq-dev && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Set work directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy requirements file and install dependencies
COPY requirements.txt /app/
RUN pip install -r requirements.txt

# Copy application code
COPY . /app/

# Expose the port the app runs on
EXPOSE 8000

# Command to run the application
CMD ["python", "manage.py", "runserver", "0.0.0.0:8000"]
